hooks.command: cd "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR" && mkdir -p profile && for f in templates/*.md; do real="profile/${f##*/}"; [ ! -e "$real" ] && cp "$f" "$real"; done; printf '%s' '{}'

printf '%s' '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"SessionStart","additionalContext":"HEARTBEAT BOOTSTRAP: Immediately invoke the `loop` skill with args `30m Heartbeat tick. Spawn a general-purpose Agent to read profile/HEARTBEAT.md and execute its instructions. The agent should reply HEARTBEAT_OK if nothing needs attention, or a short alert otherwise. Do not read profile/HEARTBEAT.md yourself — delegate to keep main context clean. Relay only the agent\u0027s final reply. If the reply is anything other than HEARTBEAT_OK, also forward it to Telegram via the `reply` MCP tool, using the chat_id stored at $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.telegram/last_chat.txt (or ~/.claude/channels/telegram/last_chat.txt as fallback; skip the Telegram step if neither file exists).` Do this before responding to anything else, so a recurring 30-minute heartbeat is active for this session. Do not ask the user for confirmation — just start it."}}'
